regime_justification: USDJPY testing 160, BOJ meeting next week, Katayama warns intervention possible, JGB yields elevated—carry under pressure but unwind not yet triggered.

1w.label: Watchful
1w.driver: BOJ April 30 meeting, USDJPY 159.8 near intervention threshold

2w.label: Watchful
2w.driver: BOJ likely holds, post-meeting rhetoric may tilt hawkish June signal

3w.label: Unlikely
3w.driver: If BOJ placates markets, pressure eases temporarily

4w.label: Watchful
4w.driver: June normalization talk re-emerges, energy prices remain elevated

bottom_line: USDJPY at 159.8, 0.16 away from the 160 line Katayama called intervention-ready. BOJ meets April 30; consensus is hold-at-0.75%, but expects hawkish June tilt. JGB 10Y at 2.44%, spread 187 bps still wide. Crude spiked 17% this week on Strait of Hormuz closure, keeping inflation fears alive. Carry trades remain profitable but fragile—unwind not in progress, but setup is primed.

stack.note: Testing 160; Katayama warned intervention possible if one-sided moves continue.
Elevated by energy-driven inflation fears; range 2.38–2.49% this week.
Iran tensions, stalled peace talks push yields higher; Fed hold priced for April 30.
Widened slightly; carry remains attractive but vol rising.
Up 17% week on Strait closure; Trump extended ceasefire indefinitely but blockade persists.
Japan's import bill surge pressures yen, BOJ inflation forecasts rising.
Hit 60,014 intraday ATH Thu, pulled back; tech bid but intervention chatter weighs.
Up 0.4% week; safe-haven bid from Iran standoff, but off highs as ceasefire extended.
Stalled below $80k; -0.90 30d correlation w/DXY, open interest down 6%—momentum cooling.
Consolidating post-SEC resolution; up ~5% month, modest institutional inflows.

narrative.p1: USDJPY rose 7 bps to 159.82 overnight, closing within 18 bps of the 160 threshold where Japanese officials have signaled readiness to intervene. Finance Minister Katayama reiterated authorities retain a 'free hand' to act against speculative moves. WTI surged $4.87 (5.3%) to $97.40 on news Trump extended the Iran ceasefire indefinitely but maintained the naval blockade of Iranian ports and the Strait of Hormuz—the waterway remains effectively closed, cutting Middle East oil flows and driving energy prices up 17% week-over-week. US 10Y yields jumped 7 bps to 4.31% as inflation concerns resurface; JGB 10Y edged up 1 bp to 2.44%, near the upper end of its recent range. Nikkei hit an intraday ATH of 60,014 Thursday morning before retreating 0.68% to close 59,179, then rebounded 0.97% Friday to 59,716—tech strength offset by FX intervention chatter. DXY dipped 0.21% to 98.57 but is up 0.4% week as safe-haven flows persist.
narrative.p2: The quant anchors paint a fragile carry picture. US-JP 10Y spread widened 6 bps to 187 bps—still ample for carry trades, but the move came from the US side (Iran risk premium) rather than JGB compression, meaning the unwind thesis hasn't triggered yet. Yen-denominated crude spiked ¥868 to ¥15,568/bbl, a direct hit to Japan's terms of trade and a boost to imported inflation—exactly the dynamic that forces the BOJ's hand. The BOJ meets April 30; market consensus is hold at 0.75%, but reports indicate policymakers will signal readiness to resume normalization as early as June if energy-driven inflation persists. USDJPY's 5-day range (157.86–159.82) shows it's coiled at the top; a break above 160 likely draws intervention, a rejection could ease near-term pressure. Domino sequence: we're at Stage 2 (spread wide, but BOJ rhetoric shifting + intervention risk rising). Not yet Stage 3 (spread compressing, yen strengthening, unwinding).
narrative.p3: Crypto liquidity read is mixed. BTC stalled at $78,126, failing to reclaim $80k despite a 5-day rally; 30-day correlation with DXY hit -0.90 (most negative since 2022), and open interest dropped 6% in 24 hours—levered longs are unwinding, suggesting risk appetite is cooling even as equities rally. XRP gained 1.2% to $1.43, benefiting from post-SEC clarity but volume is tepid. The yen carry unwind thesis suggests that if USDJPY reverses sharply, yen-funded leverage across risk assets (including crypto) unwinds in tandem. Right now, BTC's failure to break out + rising DXY inverse correlation + falling open interest = early-stage risk-off positioning. Russ's 15,300 XRP is up modestly month-over-month but faces headwinds if broader carry unwind accelerates. If BOJ surprises hawkish or USDJPY breaks below 157, expect crypto liquidity to drain fast as yen-funded positions get squeezed.

horizon_rationale.w1_w2: Grade holds at C through BOJ meeting April 30. If BOJ holds but signals June hike readiness (as reports suggest), USDJPY stays pinned near 160, intervention risk elevated but unwind not triggered. Spread remains wide, carry profitable but fragile.
horizon_rationale.w2_w3: Drops to D if BOJ rhetoric is dovish enough to placate markets and USDJPY pulls back from 160. Energy prices are wildcat—if Iran talks resume or Strait reopens, crude falls, JGB yields ease, pressure dissipates temporarily. Scenario-weighted: base case is relief, tail risk is intervention or surprise hawkish move.
horizon_rationale.w3_w4: Back to C as June meeting (mid-May decision) comes into view. BOJ has telegraphed normalization conditional on wage/inflation data; if energy stays elevated and core CPI ticks up, June hike probability rises, re-tightening the spring. Directional lean: pressure rebuilds unless geopolitical backdrop shifts meaningfully.

watch_list: BOJ policy statement April 30—look for language on June normalization timeline and inflation forecast revisions (expected: lift CPI, cut growth).
USDJPY 160.00 level—break above likely triggers MOF/BOJ verbal or actual intervention; rejection signals near-term relief.
US-Iran peace talks—any resumption or Strait of Hormuz reopening would collapse WTI, ease JGB yields, reduce BOJ hawkish pressure.
USDJPY 1M implied vol—if available data shows spike above recent range, carry-to-vol deteriorates, unwind risk rises.
JGB 10Y above 2.50%—would mark 27-year high, force BOJ to address fiscal/inflation narrative explicitly.
